headline: Transcript: Budget, boats and a by-election
summary: George Parker talks to Jennifer Williams, Jim Pickard and Robert Shrimsley
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Political Fix podcast episode: ‘Budget, boats and a by-election’

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Rachel Reeves voice clipOur economy is not broken, but I do accept that for too many people, it’s not working as it should — that has to change. And at the Budget next month . . .

George ParkerThe chancellor there, addressing this week’s first ever Regional Investment Summit in Birmingham, all part of her month-long marathon to balance the books ahead of the November 26th Budget. And for once, there was a small bit of good news for Rachel Reeves after the publication of softer-than-expected inflation stats, feeding through crucially into lower government borrowing costs.

So the focus shifted to another government department, the Home Office, hit by bad headline after bad headline from the migrant mess-up to the implosion of the grooming gang’s inquiry.

Kemi Badenoch voice clipFour victims on the rape gang’s survivors panel have resigned. And they’ve resigned because
